subtasks.description: Evaluate emergency access request from senior-engineer u_9981 to production database db-payments-primary against access control and incident response policies. Determine approval/denial and any required conditions (audit logging, time limits, scope restrictions).

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask at mid tier: The query explicitly requests governance evaluation of an emergency access request against policies. This requires multi-factor reasoning: (1) verifying the user's role eligibility for emergency escalation, (2) confirming incident severity justifies production database access, (3) applying access control policies with time-bound and audit-logging conditions typical of emergency procedures, and (4) determining approval with constraints. Mid tier is appropriate for this policy reasoning with multiple applicable rules and exception handling. No cross-recon needed (no data reconciliation required). No additional subtasks: the policy engine's output IS the required decision."

output.reason: Senior-engineer u_9981 has an active P1 incident ticket (INC-20241215-0042) which satisfies POL-501's incident ticket requirement. POL-502's dual approval requirement is waived under the declared P1 incident exception. POL-503 requires mandatory audit logging and post-incident review within 24 hours. Access is granted with time-limited, audited, and scoped constraints commensurate with the P1 emergency.
applied_constraints: All read/write operations must be fully audit-logged in real time with association to INC-20241215-0042
Emergency access window is time-limited to 4 hours from grant; renewal requires re-evaluation
Access scope restricted to tables and schemas directly relevant to the P1 incident investigation; bulk exports prohibited
Session must be terminated immediately upon P1 incident resolution or access window expiry
Post-incident access review required within 24 hours by DBA team and security team per POL-503
Any data modifications must be documented with justification before execution
Concurrent notification sent to on-call DBA and security operations team as compensating control for waived dual-approval
